title: feat(goal-guard): Stop gate blocking work punted to a 'next session' (revert #349 keyword rule)
body: New Stop-hook gate sidecar goal-guard stop-check — catches an agent PUNTING the work to a future session ('이건 다음 세션에서 이어가겠습니다' / 'next session') instead of continuing now, and blocks that stop so the model presses on. This is the actual thing the goal-support request wanted (NOT the reverted #349 keyword rule, which is removed here). Scans the last assistant message's TAIL (last ~8 non-empty lines) for future-session deferral phrases (다음 세션/추후 세션/next|future|later|another session …); a mid-message mention in analysis does not trip it. Emits decision:block with a reason telling the agent to continue now unless a CONCRETE blocker (external dep/CI wait/human approval/another machine) genuinely requires another session — in which case state the blocker and record the resume point via sidecar ing next, then stop (commons session-terminal stays valid). Single-fire via native stop_hook_active, so a genuine multi-session terminal that restates and re-stops passes through on the 2nd stop; correct operation never trips it (no Stop-hook-error noise). CC-only (Pi has no blocking Stop). Wired on BOTH surfaces: hooks/hooks.json Stop[] + modules/setup.ts Stop[] + install-hooks --global (verified live in ~/.claude/settings.json). QA: defer-in-tail→block, no-defer→pass, mid-mention-outside-tail→pass, blocker-1st-stop→block(nudge once), stop_hook_active→pass, no-transcript→pass, bad-verb exit1, help exit0.

title: feat fable-mode
body: fable-mode: DELEGATE 목록에 난제(HARD PROBLEMS — 막힌 문제·실패한 시도·까다로운 버그/증명/알고리즘) 추가 — directive·on/status 메시지·ARCHITECTURE 노드 lockstep 갱신

title: feat(keywords): goal-continuity rule — route 다음/잔여/다세션 continuation keywords to /goal + ing (CC goal support)
body: Support Claude Code's native /goal feature via a keyword-router rule (design delegated to Fable 5 per fable-mode split policy; implemented locally). New config/keywords.json rule goal-continuity catches continuation-intent phrases — 다음 진행/다음 세션/잔여/남은 작업/이어서/계속 진행 + next step/remaining work/keep going/what's next/continue from/\\bresumes?\\b — and its hint routes progress: single-session → pull remaining from sidecar ing (next) or sidecar go, long-horizon → native /goal <condition> or stop after N turns (auto-continues each turn, restored on --resume), multi-session wall → sidecar ing next <resume-point> then STOP (session-terminal). NO new sidecar command: /goal state is CC-session-internal (a wrapper would be a blind echo = redundant-wrapper violation) and Pi has no /goal (agent-neutral). Role split from session-handoff: handoff=ending→record(write), goal-continuity=continuing→consume(read). Phrase-only patterns (no bare 다음/next/continue) to avoid false positives. Data-only rule → auto-applies on BOTH surfaces (CC UserPromptSubmit + Pi) via existing prompt-scan, zero engine diff, advisory (not per-turn inject budget). QA: 7 positive MATCH, 5 negative NOMATCH (다음과 같이/continue-on-error/next.js/계속 실패/잔뜩 all correctly skip).

title: feat(fable-mode): split policy — delegate DESIGN/ANALYSIS to Fable 5, implement LOCALLY
body: Rework fable-mode into a SPLIT delegation policy (per user: '설계·분석 등만 fable, 실제진행은 기본진행'). Previously ON delegated ALL substantive work (incl. implementation) to Fable 5; now the per-turn directive splits by KIND: DELEGATE the thinking (design · architecture · analysis · root-cause investigation · research · review · planning/spec) to Fable 5 via sidecar fable, but DO the implementation LOCALLY yourself — writing/editing code, builds/tests, git/worktree, commit, ship, and all repo bookkeeping (ARCHITECTURE/ING/CHANGELOG). Rationale: Fable 5's depth pays off on design/investigation, while headless delegation of code changes carries real friction (read-only default #347, context overhead, --json blindness, worktree stalls). Updated DIRECTIVE + on/status messages + module header + cli HELP + ARCHITECTURE node. OFF still emits nothing. QA: help parses, ON inject emits split directive (DELEGATE design + DO LOCALLY impl both present).

title: feat(fable): add --write (bypassPermissions) — headless fable is read-only by default; opt into full write/execute
body: New flag sidecar fable --write (aliases --bypass/--agent) — grants WRITE/EXECUTE by injecting --permission-mode bypassPermissions into the child claude -p (Write/Edit/Bash/git, no approval prompts). Root cause it fixes: headless claude -p runs in the default permission mode with no human to approve prompts, so Write/Edit/Bash/git calls are AUTO-DENIED — a delegated fable child is effectively READ-ONLY (Read/Grep/Glob + text .result) out of the box. This is why an 'implement X in a worktree' delegation reads+plans forever and never creates the worktree (git worktree add silently denied; --json hides the denial). --write makes fable a full unattended implementer. DEFAULT stays read-only (safe); write is opt-in per user request (모두 허용/bypass). Injected before o.extra so a user's own -- --permission-mode still wins (can narrow to acceptEdits or allowlist tools). --dry shows write=on(bypassPermissions)|write=off(read-only). QA: default no permission-mode, --write→bypassPermissions, aliases, user override wins, regression 18/18 + edge 41/41.

title: feat(fable): add 'fable tail <id>' — follow a bg job's output stream live (watch progress)
body: New verb sidecar fable tail <id> — follows a background job's output stream LIVE until it finishes (the missing 'watch progress' view, alongside result/wait/list). Re-reads the small job out file each ~300ms and writes only the new tail; returns when the exitcode file appears, printing a done/exit trailer (exit = the job's code, 1 on no-such-job). A streaming non-json job fills out incrementally so you see it type; a --json job emits one blob at the end so tail stays quiet until done (launch without --json or with -- --output-format stream-json to watch it stream). Ctrl-C stops the VIEW not the detached job. 'watch' is an alias. QA: help parses, no-such-job exit1, live-follow of a real bg job prints incrementally then done trailer, re-tail of an already-done job dumps full out + trailer.

title: feat(fable): forbid Opus fallback unconditionally — pin availableModels allowlist (no opus) so flagged requests refuse on the delegated model
body: sidecar fable now FORBIDS Opus fallback unconditionally (not an opt-in flag — per user: '선택가능 옵션이 아니라 fallback 금지'). Root cause researched via web + official model-config docs: Fable 5 runs cybersecurity/biology safety classifiers; when a request is flagged, Claude Code's default re-runs it on the default Opus model (Opus 4.8), and it can trip on the FIRST request from workspace context alone (CLAUDE.md + git status) — which is why delegated/workflow runs silently 'turn into Opus'. Fix: fable always launches the child with --settings '{"availableModels":[<model>,"sonnet","haiku"]}'. With Opus outside the allowlist the docs guarantee 'no fallback occurs; the refusal is shown as a normal error and the session's model is unchanged' — the run stays on the delegated model, a flagged request refuses instead of becoming Opus. Allowlist always includes the chosen -m model so overrides work; user's own -- --settings still wins (injected before o.extra). --dry shows opus-fallback=off. Overload fallback separately off (never passes --fallback-model). QA: dry injects allowlist, -m override reflected, live call modelUsage shows fable-5 only (no opus), regression 18/18 + edge 41/41.

title: feat(fable-mode): session toggle — ON delegates substantive work to Fable 5 via sidecar fable (per-turn inject · 3-surface wired)
body: New command sidecar fable-mode {on|off|status|inject} [--repo] — a session-scoped toggle that, when ON, emits a per-turn UserPromptSubmit directive mandating the agent DELEGATE this turn's substantive work (code/analysis/design/research) to the Fable 5 model via sidecar fable (file-mediated), keeping orchestration + repo bookkeeping local and barring recursion. Scope mirrors recommend: repo .harness/fable-mode > host ~/.sidecar/fable-mode (bare on/off = host-wide, --repo = committed/team). OFF (default) emits NOTHING → zero aggregate inject-budget cost for non-users; the ON directive is in-code + conditional (recommend defaultDirective precedent), no injectCap needed. Wired on all 3 surfaces: setup.ts UserPromptSubmit (live settings.json SSOT) + hooks/hooks.json (plugin) + pi/sidecar.ts PER_TURN. Answers the repo-vs-session scope question: sidecar has no native session scope, so host-wide is the practical 'this agent now' toggle. QA: OFF inject empty, ON emits JSON directive, status/on/off/--repo, bad-verb exit1, help exit0, help parses (index-ts-1).

title: chore(fable): retire the run-timeout default — unlimited unless --timeout <s> opt-in
body: Retire the fable run-timeout DEFAULT — a run is now UNLIMITED unless an explicit --timeout <s> is passed. Reverts the #342 30-min default. The stall it guarded (headless child inheriting global governance Stop-hooks, looping) is already fixed at the source by the default --sources project,local (#327), so an auto-cap only risks killing a legitimately long generation. --timeout <s> stays as an opt-in reaper (foreground kill / --bg watchdog -> exit 124); --timeout 0 also = unlimited. QA: default dry timeout=off, --timeout 60 caps, regression 18/18.

title: feat(fable): async --bg + result/wait/list jobs · --timeout default 1800s (30min, bg watchdog)
body: Two fable improvements. (1) Async mode: --bg launches the claude child DETACHED and returns a job id immediately (no blocking wait); collect with 'sidecar fable result <id>' (prints output if done, else RUNNING/exit3), 'wait <id> [--timeout s]' (block until done), 'list'. Jobs under ~/.sidecar/fable-jobs/<id>/ (prompt·out·err·exitcode = done signal). This replaces the hand-rolled 'sidecar fable … & + pgrep poll' pattern (which self-matches its own command line → false RUNNING, the remote-poll-pgrep trap). (2) --timeout now DEFAULTS to 1800s (30 min) for both foreground AND --bg (a shell watchdog SIGKILLs a stalled bg job → exitcode 124, self-reaping); --timeout 0 = unlimited; negative rejected. QA: default 1800/off/override/reject, bg launch→result RUNNING(exit3)→wait collects answer, list, watchdog status; regression 18/18 + edge sweep.

title: fix(ship): run shadow subshell from homedir (swept-worktree dead cwd broke command -v)
body: Second-layer fix for ship's shadow step after the worktree self-sweep. Prior fix (#340) ran shadow via the global 'sidecar shadow', but the ship process's OWN cwd is the worktree pr-cycle just swept — so the spawned subshell inherited a deleted cwd, getcwd failed, and 'command -v sidecar' mis-fired into the __NO_GLOBAL_SIDECAR__ fallback (in-process runShadow against the gone worktree → 'no source commands'). Fix: run that subshell with cwd=homedir() (a stable dir), so PATH resolves and the global sidecar shadow mirrors from the just-updated ~/.sidecar/cli regardless of the swept worktree.

title: fix(ship): run shadow via global binary so it survives pr-cycle's worktree self-sweep
body: Harden ship's shadow step against the pr-cycle self-sweep. Now that pr-cycle deterministically removes the worktree it merged from (#338/#339), when 'sidecar ship' runs from a worktree, pr-cycle (step 2) sweeps that worktree — so ship's in-process runShadow (step 4) loses its SIDECAR_ROOT/commands source and fails ('no source commands'). Fix: run shadow via the GLOBAL binary ('sidecar shadow') after self-update (step 3) has already refreshed ~/.sidecar/cli — it mirrors from the just-updated global clone, surviving the self-sweep. Falls back to in-process runShadow only if the global sidecar isn't on PATH. Observed live: ship from wt-* self-swept the worktree and shadow then failed needing a manual re-run; this closes that gap.

title: fix(pr-cycle): deterministically sweep the just-merged branch's worktree (remote ref lingered from worktree merges → never [gone])
body: Make pr-cycle's post-merge worktree cleanup DETERMINISTIC instead of relying on the [gone] heuristic. Root cause of pile-up: when pr-cycle merges from a linked worktree, gh's --delete-branch post-step is a LOCAL checkout that fails (base held by main worktree), so the REMOTE branch often lingers and the local branch never reads as [gone] (upstream track stays []). The sweep only removed [gone] worktrees → the just-merged worktree was never cleaned and accumulated (anima hit 46). Fix: pr-cycle passes the branch it just VERIFIED as merged into sweepMergedWorktrees(mergedBranch); the sweep explicitly deletes the lingering remote ref (git push origin --delete, best-effort — works from a worktree, no local checkout), fetches -p, and sweeps a worktree when its branch is [gone] OR is the verified-merged branch (heuristic-independent). Combined with #338 (path filter dropped + dirty-preserve), every verified pr-cycle now auto-removes the worktree it ran in. Only passes the branch when the merge verified on base (onBase).

title: feat(pr-cycle): sweep merged worktrees at ANY path (not just .claude/worktrees) + preserve dirty ones
body:
Improve pr-cycle's post-merge worktree sweep so worktrees stop piling up. The sweep already ff-synced main then removed merged ([gone]) linked worktrees, but it was gated to '.claude/worktrees/' agent worktrees only — so a manually-added 'git worktree add -b
' (the standard isolated-worktree pattern this project uses everywhere) was NEVER reaped and accumulated (e.g. anima hit 46 worktrees). Fix: drop the path restriction — sweep EVERY linked worktree whose branch is [gone], regardless of path. Added safety the old code lacked: a worktree with UNCOMMITTED changes is now PRESERVED with a warning instead of being --force-clobbered (commit/discard then 'sidecar worktree gc'). Still never touches main, locked worktrees, or branches with live/absent upstream. Net: after every verified merge, the worktree pr-cycle ran in (and any sibling merged worktree) is auto-removed — no more manual 'git worktree remove'.

title: fix(inject): toolkit + companions catalogs emit via stdout hookSpecificOutput (were stderr → silently dropped, agent never saw commands)
body: ROOT-CAUSE fix: the sidecar command catalog (toolkit inject) and neighbour-CLI surface (companions inject) were written to process.stderr as raw text — a channel Claude Code does NOT read as SessionStart additionalContext — so BOTH catalogs silently never reached the agent. Symptom: an agent told to 'use the fable command' had no idea it existed (every sidecar command looked unknown), because the proactive catalog was dropped every session. Fix: both inject subcommands now read the hook payload from STDIN and emit via emitInject() → stdout {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName,additionalContext}} JSON, the exact schema commons/recommend/architecture already use and that Claude Code reads. A bare manual run (no stdin JSON) stays silent as before. Verified: printf hook-payload | sidecar toolkit inject → stdout JSON with 9855-char catalog incl. fable + hypotheses; companions same. Convergence inject-ts-2 records the stderr-drop trap. Note: existing sessions must reload/restart to receive the now-delivered catalog.

title: fix(fable): reject unknown flags with a clear error instead of swallowing them as prompt words
body: Fix fable UX bug (field-hit by another session): an unrecognized flag was silently swallowed as a positional prompt word. Two failure modes: (a) '--file X --setting-sources project,local' tripped the misleading 'prompt sources are exclusive' error (the flag+value counted as argv words, conflicting with --file); (b) '--dry --setting-sources project,local hi' silently folded the unknown flag INTO the prompt (34 chars). Common trigger: claude's own --setting-sources vs fable's --sources. Fix: parseArgs now REJECTS any unrecognized token starting with '-' (except bare '-' stdin) with a clear 'unknown flag' error listing the known flags + reminding that claude's own flags go after '--'. QA: --setting-sources → unknown-flag error+exit1; correct --sources still parses; '-- --setting-sources x' passthrough intact; all known flags (-m/--json/--cwd/--timeout/-c/--sources) parse; regression 18/18; help parses.

title: commons(fanout-workflow): retire Fable-5 workflow-model clauses (workflow {model:fable}→opus, guidance was wrong)
body: Retire the Fable-5 fan-out clauses from the commons fanout-workflow rule (added in #334). In practice a Workflow agent spawned with {model:'fable'} is auto-switched to opus, so the guidance ('spawn Workflow agents with {model:fable}' / 'loop-one-at-a-time sidecar fable when ≥3 fit a Workflow') was misleading. Reverted to the clean pre-#334 form: fan ≥3 independent tasks into ONE Workflow call; don't fire N direct Agent calls. No fable-specific mechanics in commons.

title: feat(hypotheses): canonical HYPOTHESES/ folder convention — guard + audit + migrate + 가설 keyword
body: New hypothesis-folder convention (config-driven, cross-project): ONE canonical dir for pre-register→falsify→run→verdict science work (config hypotheses.dir, default HYPOTHESES/). Pieces: (1) config hypotheses.{dir,aliases} + hypothesisGuard toggle (default on) in lib/config.ts. (2) modules/hypothesis-guard.ts — pre write/bash code guard: BLOCKS creating a NEW stray hypothesis folder (a per-repo hypotheses.alias e.g. anima UNIVERSE, or the built-in hypothes*/가설* name pattern), WARNS on edits to an existing one (don't paralyze a not-yet-migrated repo); mv/cp dest-only so renaming AWAY to HYPOTHESES/ is allowed; @hypothesis-ok / # hypothesis-ok overrides. Wired into pre.ts (shared CLI → covers both CC + Pi surfaces). (3) modules/hypotheses.ts — sidecar hypotheses {check [--gate]|migrate <dir>|scaffold|show}: check audits the repo-root backlog, migrate = history-preserving git mv <dir>→HYPOTHESES/ (merges per-entry if it exists), scaffold creates the skeleton. (4) keywords.json hypothesis-convention trigger (가설/hypothesis/pre-register/반증/falsif). (5) commands/hypotheses.md slash delegator + HELP + ARCHITECTURE module-hypotheses node + toolkit (80). QA: help parse smoke, mkdir hypotheses_burst/mkdir 가설_2/write under hypotheses_x all BLOCK, mv <stray>→HYPOTHESES ALLOW, check clean, keyword fires. Name-pattern always-on (not marker-gated, config-ts-1).

title: refactor(commons): Tier-2 dedup — merge fix-the-tool→upstream-fix, native-canonical-first→canonical-cli; drop hexa dojo mentions
body: Tier-2 commons dedup: merged two overlapping slug pairs to cut per-turn inject bloat. (1) fix-the-tool folded INTO upstream-fix (both mandated: fix a dependent/upstream tool's CAUSE in its own canonical repo this session, verify in-session, merge via its own pr-cycle; don't cover-a-wall/patch-vendored/defer/leave-unmerged) — upstream-fix kept as the survivor slug (more wired: upstream.ts module, /upstream cmd, handoff-guard). (2) native-canonical-first folded INTO canonical-cli (use the provided command else the native primitive; no reinvent/wrapper/bypass) — canonical-cli kept (more wired: cloud-guard, pod-poll). Updated the 2 minority references (enforcement.json hint fix-the-tool->upstream-fix, naming.ts comment native-canonical-first->canonical-cli) so no dangling slug pointers. Also dropped hexa dojo mentions from canonical-cli + heavy-on-pool per request. commons 30->28 slugs, 8348->7837B; aggregate inject budget 16741/22883 green.

title: feat(commons): session-terminal rule — a blocked/multi-session goal is a valid recorded terminal, not a loop
body: New commons governance rule 'session-terminal': a goal that is blocked THIS session (needs another session, async/external dependency, human-only input, or is the endpoint of a multi-session campaign) is a VALID terminal — record the wall + exact resume target to ING/ARCHITECTURE, report once, and STOP; do NOT re-derive the same 'blocked/multi-session' verdict every turn (loop), fabricate false completion to escape a goal-loop, or treat a recorded cross-session handoff as failure. Motivation: a session hit a Claude-Code goal/loop that re-invoked 9 consecutive times because the agent kept honestly re-asserting 'can't, it's multi-session' with no governance-sanctioned honest terminal, until the native stop-hook block-cap overrode it. Sidecar's own Stop hooks were NOT the cause (all bail on stop_hook_active). Bumped lint.commonsCap 8000->8500 to fit the rule; the true context-rot ceiling (aggregate injectBudgetBytes 22883) stays green at 17252B.

title: feat(fable): first-class -c/--continue + -r/--resume <id> multi-turn continuity
body: fable gains first-class multi-turn continuity: -c/--continue (continue the most recent conversation in --cwd) and -r/--resume <session_id> (resume a specific session captured from a prior --json run's .session_id). Both make the call STATEFUL; they map to claude -p's own --continue/--resume and are mutually exclusive (fable rejects both). Sessions are keyed to --cwd. Verified this works UNDER the default hook-free --sources project,local (session persistence is not a setting source). Live e2e: seed 'codeword=천리마' via --json → captured session_id → --resume recalled 천리마 → --continue recalled it too (all exit 0). Docs: templates/fable.md Continuity section + seed/resume recipe, help line, ARCHITECTURE module-fable. QA: 18/18 regression + dry continue/resume + mutual-exclusion reject + the live recall.

title: fix(fable): default --setting-sources project,local — drop global governance hooks that stalled the headless child (exit 124)
body: Root-cause fix for the fable headless stall: the child claude -p inherits the GLOBAL ~/.claude/settings.json governance Stop-hooks; in a git repo cwd with uncommitted changes those hooks BLOCK the child after it answers (architecture/convergence stop-check → forced continue loop) so the process never exits and --timeout kills it at exit 124, and the final-turn .result is overwritten by CONVERGENCE/ARCHITECTURE boilerplate instead of the real output. Fix: fable now launches the child with --setting-sources project,local BY DEFAULT, dropping the global user source (where the hooks live) while keychain auth (not a setting source) keeps it logged in and project,local still gives repo CLAUDE.md. New --sources flag opts back into full inheritance (user,project,local). Proven A/B on the full mitosis brainstorm from --cwd anima: was exit 124 / 200s kill / num_turns 3 / boilerplate result → now exit 0 / 111s / num_turns 1 / 7.7KB clean brainstorm. Rejected claude --bare (also skips keychain → Not-logged-in). Docs: runbook templates/fable.md hook-isolation section + brainstorm recipe, help line, keywords hint, ARCHITECTURE module-fable, convergence fable-ts-1. QA: 18/18 regression + new-flag smoke + the live anima breakthrough.

title: docs(fable): agent caveat runbook (templates/fable.md) + skill pointer
body: Agent-facing caveat runbook for fable: new templates/fable.md — invocation matrix (file/argv/stdin, exactly one source), 8 hard caveats each traced to a field incident (never inline free text on a shell line · always --timeout on unattended runs, 124=auth stall · never wrap with external timeout (eaten pipe) · --json .result for clean machine output vs hook-boilerplate in prose · deliberate --cwd (child tool reach) · no recursion/unbounded fan-out · batch instead of stateless pings, -- --continue for follow-ups · background pattern with 0-byte-log = FAILED), and an exit-code table (0/1/124/127). commands/fable.md now points agents at the sheet before unattended/background use.

title: fix(fable): runbook-style /fable (file-mediated free text) + eaten-pipe stdin hint
body: Round-3 field fixes for /fable free-text safety: (1) commands/fable.md converted from a '!' one-liner to a RUNBOOK-style skill — textual $ARGUMENTS substitution into a shell line cannot be quoted safely for arbitrary free text (broke on parens, then again on double quotes); the agent now Writes the verbatim prompt to a scratch file and runs sidecar fable --file <f> --timeout 600. Convergence recorded (fable-md-1). (2) fable's empty-stdin error now explains the eaten-pipe trap (a backgrounding timeout shim points a POSIX-sh background job's stdin at /dev/null) and points to the native --timeout flag. Host-side, /usr/local/bin/timeout shim itself was fixed with an explicit <&0 redirect (stdin now survives; verified echo|timeout cat). QA: 0-byte stdin hint fires, argv-empty message unchanged, help smoke ok, runbook md has no ! line.

title: fix(fable): stdin slash delegator + --timeout stall cap + fable keyword trigger
body: Field-QA fixes for the fable command, from first real use: (1) /fable slash delegator broke on shell-special prompt text (parens/globs → bash syntax error) — now pipes $ARGUMENTS via stdin (printf '%s' | sidecar fable -, ing --stdin precedent); slash form is prompt-only, flags stay CLI-side. (2) headless claude -p can sleep forever waiting for login credentials (observed 8m24s, 0% CPU) — new --timeout <s> kills the child and exits 124 with an auth hint. (3) 'fable' keyword trigger added to config/keywords.json (fable-delegate) so prompts mentioning fable surface the delegation hint. QA: help smoke, --dry timeout echo, fake-claude stall killed at 2s (exit 124), paren prompt via stdin OK, keyword trigger fires. Plus full 18/18 QA sweep on the base command.

title: feat(fable): headless Fable 5 delegation command (claude -p)
body: New command: sidecar fable [flags] <prompt...> | --file <f> | - — delegate ONE instruction to the Fable 5 model via headless claude -p (default --model claude-fable-5, -m to override). Prompt sources are exclusive (argv words / --file / stdin '-') and are always written to the child's stdin (no argv quoting/length/history leak); stdout/stderr inherited for live streaming. Flags: --json (claude --output-format json), --dry (print resolved argv + prompt size, no run), --cwd <dir>, and everything after -- passes to claude verbatim. Wiring: modules/fable.ts + cli/index.ts register + HELP line (single quotes only — index-ts-1) + commands/fable.md slash delegator + ARCHITECTURE.json module-fable node + TOOLKIT.jsonl (79 entries). E2E verified: --json run returned result 'fable ok' with modelUsage=claude-fable-5.

title: fix models
body: model 명령을 models 로 rename — 슬래시 /model 이 Claude Code 기본 /model 과 충돌해 picker 에서 가려지던 문제 해소. CLI subcommand·slash·모듈(runModels)·commands/models.md·dataset 상호참조·ARCHITECTURE 노드·TOOLKIT 카탈로그 일괄 갱신. 데이터 키 .models[] 는 불변(네임스페이스 분리).

title: feat(architecture): add untested ⚪ pre-state to result store (registered-but-not-run · 3 directions unchanged)
body: 결과 스토어에 선행상태 untested⚪(등록됐으나 아직 안 잼) 1개 추가 — 방향 3단계(pos/in-prog/neg)는 신호등 그대로 두고, '데이터 없음'을 표현 못하던 공백만 메움. RESULT_STATES=CONV_STATES+untested (convergence 는 strict trio 유지). 세부 '왜'(막힘·불확정·무효)는 metric 자유서술. anima G0~G6 처럼 미측정 게이트를 정직하게 구분 가능.

title: refactor(architecture): unify neg-conv icon 🧱→🔴 (reserve 🧱 for break-walls terminal verdict)
body: convergence-state 의 neg-conv 아이콘을 🧱→🔴 로 통일 — 수렴상태 네거티브는 🔴(음성 결과), 🧱은 break-walls 종결벽(다중렌즈 소진 후) 전용으로 분리. convStateLabel 한 곳만 변경(results 와 공유) · 중복 resultStateLabel 제거 · ARCHITECTURE.json convergence/results note+node · 코드주석

title: feat(architecture): bench/experiment result accumulation store (result subcommand · 3-stage convergence-state)
body: architecture 에 벤치·실험 결과 누적 스토어 추가 — 3단계 수렴상태(pos-conv/in-prog/neg-conv) 공유. convergence 재발학습 store 의 누적 형제: sidecar architecture result {list [--kind bench|experiment]|for <subject>|add --kind <k> --subject <s> --value <v> [--state|--metric|--new|--id]|edit|rm}. 별도 top-level results.records[] 스토어(id·kind·state·value·metric·subject) · convergence 와 달리 file-touch 표면화·Stop게이트 없음(순수 누적 · falsified/음성 결과 보존 · honesty·preserve-state) · lint RESULT-MALFORMED 게이트(id+valid kind+valid state) · 트리 hygiene skip · CC/Pi 무관(엔진 CLI 공유, hook 미배선)

title: feat prefs
body: add prefs stop-check — a turn-close WARN Stop gate (never blocks) enforcing the prefs language axes the per-edit guards can't cover: DOCS axis (working-tree diff of doc files .md/README/CHANGELOG authored this turn with Korean prose, backtick code-spans exempt) and RESPONSE axis (heuristic: reply prose almost entirely non-Hangul while prefs.response=korean). CODE axis stays covered per-edit by codeLangViolation (post.ts) so it is NOT re-checked here (avoids double-nag + Korean-string-literal false positives). Wired on Stop in both hooks/hooks.json and setup.ts (global settings); CC-only like other Stop gates. Inline last-assistant reader avoids a circular import with recommend.ts. Escalates prefs from advisory-inject-only to enforced-warn; hard block stays only on re-injected governance docs.
